title: Branchwater Table Actions — a DB editor with snapshot-undo
overview: Turn the bw ui table browser into a real DB editor: inline row edit/insert/delete, truncate & drop, a SQL console, and CSV/JSON export. Every mutating action auto-snapshots the pre-change state first and surfaces one-click Undo (restore that snapshot) — a safety net other DB GUIs can't offer. Writes go through a new OPTIONAL, engine-agnostic MutableAdapter capability (Postgres implements it); engines without it stay read-only and the core never imports an adapter.
scopeChallenge: EXPANSION confirmed with the user. Large net-new WRITE surface spanning every layer (capability -> adapter -> orchestrator -> server -> frontend), destructive (mutates live DBs), so comprehensive tests + safety are warranted. The four action sets (row CRUD, SQL console, table ops, export) and the auto-snapshot+undo safety model were chosen by the user. Heavy reuse of existing code: the optional-capability pattern (InspectableAdapter/MaterializableAdapter + guards), orchestrator snapshot/checkout/contextFor/adapter.restore, the register*Routes + token + confirm-gate server pattern, and the React tab/component shell. Ruled out: schema-migration UI, multi-row bulk edit, cross-action transactions, new runtime deps; Postgres only (capability is engine-agnostic, second engine is future work). Overriding constraints: engine-agnostic boundary holds (core/cli-commands/server never import adapters; arch test scans src/server recursively so new routes are auto-covered); no SQL injection (quoteIdent identifiers, safely-quoted/typed values, SQL on psql stdin, secrets via PGPASSWORD); every write is confirm:true + token gated and produces an undo snapshot.

failureModes.risk: SQL injection via row values / identifiers
failureModes.mitigation: quoteIdent identifiers + typed/quoted literals; SQL on stdin; injection unit test (TASK-020)

failureModes.risk: An UPDATE/DELETE with no WHERE mutates the whole table
failureModes.mitigation: Adapter + endpoint reject an empty where; PK-based match preferred; tested (020/022)
Auto-snapshot BEFORE every write; one-click Undo via restoreSnapshot (007/011/014)
Orchestrator + server use isMutable guard + capability contract only; arch test scans src/server

failureModes.risk: Mutate called on a non-mutable engine
failureModes.mitigation: isMutable guard -> typed 4xx error, not a TypeError; tested (021/022)

failureModes.risk: SQL console returns an unbounded/huge result
failureModes.mitigation: Cap returned rows; execute bounds the grid; document the cap
Safety autosave before restore (mirrors checkout); partial-failure reporting; tested (021)

failureModes.risk: Row identity ambiguous without a primary key
failureModes.mitigation: Report PK from introspection; fall back to full-row NULL-safe match; auto-snapshot makes any over-match recoverable
Quote-aware CSV parser; non-SELECT -> command-tag path

failureModes.risk: Auto-snapshot before EVERY write is a full pg_dump — slow/disk-heavy on large databases
failureModes.mitigation: Reuses the existing pg_dump -Fc; suits dev DBs and is documented; consider a session-level/debounced 'one undo point per edit session' instead of per-save as a follow-up

failureModes.risk: SQL console execute() can run long-locking or multi-statement SQL
failureModes.mitigation: Documented as a power-user tool (the user owns the SQL); confirm-gated + token-gated + auto-snapshot; cap returned rows

testCoverageMap.codepath: Postgres mutate builders (quoting, NULL WHERE, empty-where refusal)
Write endpoints: confirm gate + token + empty-where + undo id
Real PG insert/update/delete/truncate/sql + undo round trip
bw ui insert -> undo through the running server

tasks.title: MutableAdapter capability + isMutable guard + table primary key
tasks.description: Extend src/core/adapter/types.ts: add MutationResult ({command, rowCount, columns?, rows?}), RowValues, RowMatch, MutableAdapter (execute/insertRow/updateRow/deleteRow/truncateTable/dropTable) + isMutable(a) guard; add OPTIONAL primaryKey?: string[] to TableInfo. Pure types + guard, no engine imports.
acceptanceCriteria: MutableAdapter, MutationResult, and isMutable compile under strict mode with zero adapter imports
isMutable narrows an object missing any mutate method to false
TableInfo.primaryKey is optional so existing inspections without it still typecheck

tasks.title: Mutation/SQL/restore DTOs
tasks.description: Extend src/server/dto.ts with SqlReqDTO/SqlResDTO, InsertRowReqDTO/UpdateRowReqDTO/DeleteRowReqDTO, TruncateReqDTO/DropReqDTO, RestoreReqDTO, and MutationResDTO ({result?, undoSnapshotId?, state}). All write requests require confirm:true. Reuse ColumnInfo/StateDTO.
acceptanceCriteria: Every new write endpoint has a request DTO requiring confirm:true
Mutation responses carry both refreshed state and an undoSnapshotId
DTOs reuse existing ColumnInfo/StateDTO, not redefining them

tasks.title: Postgres mutate SQL builder
tasks.description: Create src/adapters/postgres/mutate.ts: build+run (exec/psql on stdin, buildPgEnv creds) INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE/DROP with quoteIdent identifiers and typed quoteValue literals (numbers/booleans bare, NULL for null, single-quote-doubled strings) so untrusted values can't inject. execute(sql) runs an arbitrary statement via psql --csv and returns {command,rowCount,columns?,rows?} (parse CSV for result rows, command tag otherwise). Build WHERE from a RowMatch (NULL -> IS NULL).
acceptanceCriteria: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE/DROP build with quoteIdent identifiers and safely-quoted values
A value like o'); DROP TABLE x; -- is quoted as a literal, never executed
execute('SELECT 1 AS n') returns columns+rows; execute('DELETE ...') returns the command tag + rowCount

tasks.title: PostgresAdapter implements MutableAdapter
tasks.description: Modify src/adapters/postgres/index.ts so PostgresAdapter implements EngineAdapter, InspectableAdapter, MaterializableAdapter, MutableAdapter, delegating the six methods to mutate.ts (normalize via normalizePgConnection). Build WHERE for update/delete from the provided match.
acceptanceCriteria: isMutable(new PostgresAdapter()) is true
insertRow/updateRow/deleteRow/truncateTable/dropTable/execute invoke the mutate.ts builders
An updateRow/deleteRow with an empty where is rejected (refuses to touch every row)

tasks.title: Report primary keys in Postgres introspection
tasks.description: Modify src/adapters/postgres/introspect.ts inspect() to also query primary-key columns (pg_index/pg_attribute or information_schema.key_column_usage) as JSON and populate TableInfo.primaryKey per table (omitted when none). Exclude system schemas like the existing queries.
acceptanceCriteria: A table with a PK reports its key column(s) in primaryKey (ordered)
A table with no PK omits primaryKey rather than erroring
The PK query excludes system schemas
test/adapters/postgres-introspect.test.ts is extended to assert primaryKey is parsed for a keyed table and omitted for a PK-less one

tasks.title: Orchestrator mutation methods
tasks.description: Modify src/core/orchestrator.ts: add executeSql/insertRow/updateRow/deleteRow/truncateTable/dropTable (engine, ...) that resolve the adapter via the registry, narrow with isMutable, and call through; throw a clear 'engine "<name>" does not support writes' error otherwise. Import only the capability interface, never an adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: Each method delegates to the resolved adapter for a mutable engine
A non-mutable engine yields a clear typed error (not a TypeError on a missing method)
orchestrator.ts still imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: Orchestrator restoreSnapshot (undo)
tasks.description: Modify src/core/orchestrator.ts: add restoreSnapshot(snapshotId) -> {autosaveId, restored, failed}. Look up the snapshot record, take a safety autosave of current state first (reuse the snapshotInto/checkout pattern), then adapter.restore each engine to that snapshot's per-engine id, reporting restored vs failed. Engine-agnostic (uses the existing adapter.restore contract).
acceptanceCriteria: restoreSnapshot(id) restores all engines to that snapshot's per-engine ids
A safety autosave is captured before any restore (recoverable on partial failure)
An unknown snapshotId throws a clear error without touching any database

tasks.title: SQL console endpoint
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/sql.ts: POST /api/engines/:name/sql ({sql, confirm:true}). Reject without confirm; auto-snapshot via orchestrator.snapshot('before SQL console run'); then orchestrator.executeSql(name, sql); respond {result, undoSnapshotId, state}. Map non-mutable/unknown engine to 4xx. Bound returned row count.
acceptanceCriteria: A run without confirm:true returns 400 and does not execute or snapshot
A successful run returns the result grid + an undoSnapshotId
An engine that isn't mutable returns 400 not_writable, not a 500

tasks.title: Row insert/update/delete endpoints
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/mutate.ts: POST /api/engines/:name/tables/:table/rows (insert), .../rows/update ({where,set,confirm}), .../rows/delete ({where,confirm}). Each: reject without confirm; auto-snapshot first; call the matching orchestrator row method; respond {result, undoSnapshotId, state}. Reject update/delete with empty where. Honor ?schema=.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert/update/delete each require confirm:true (400 otherwise, no DB touched)
Update/delete with an empty/missing where return 400 (never mutate all rows)
Each success returns an undoSnapshotId and the refreshed state

tasks.title: Truncate / drop table endpoints
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/tableops.ts: POST /api/engines/:name/tables/:table/truncate and .../drop ({confirm:true}, honor ?schema=). Reject without confirm; auto-snapshot first; call orchestrator.truncateTable/dropTable; respond {undoSnapshotId, state}.
acceptanceCriteria: Truncate/drop require confirm:true (400 otherwise, no DB touched)
Each captures an undoSnapshotId before the operation
An unknown table surfaces a 4xx error, not a 500

tasks.title: Restore / undo endpoint
tasks.description: Create src/server/routes/restore.ts: POST /api/restore ({snapshotId, confirm:true}) -> orchestrator.restoreSnapshot(snapshotId); respond {result:{autosaveId,restored,failed}, state}. Reject without confirm; map an unknown snapshot to 4xx.
acceptanceCriteria: Restore requires confirm:true (400 otherwise)
A valid snapshotId restores and returns the refreshed state
An unknown snapshotId returns 4xx, not a 500

tasks.title: Mount the new routes
tasks.description: Modify src/server/server.ts to registerSqlRoutes, registerMutateRoutes, registerTableOpsRoutes, registerRestoreRoutes onto the router (alongside ops/inspect/diff), passing the injected orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: All new routes respond (e.g. POST /api/restore reaches its handler)
Existing routes (state/inspect/diff) still work
server.ts imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: API client methods for mutations + restore
tasks.description: Modify web/src/api.ts: add executeSql, insertRow, updateRow, deleteRow, truncateTable, dropTable, restore — each POSTs with confirm:true and the token, typed against the TASK-002 DTOs (type-only @bw/dto). Honor optional schema via query.
acceptanceCriteria: Every method posts confirm:true and attaches the session token
Methods are typed against the new DTOs (compile-checked)
A 4xx/5xx surfaces BwApiError.message like the existing methods

tasks.title: Undo context + Undo banner
tasks.description: Create web/src/undo.tsx (React context/provider + useUndo() hook exposing recordUndo(snapshotId,label) and the current entry) and web/src/components/UndoBanner.tsx (appears after a mutation; Undo calls api.restore then refreshes; dismissable).
acceptanceCriteria: recordUndo makes the banner appear with the snapshot id/label
Clicking Undo calls api.restore and clears the banner on success
An Undo failure surfaces an error and keeps the banner (retryable)

tasks.title: Add-row form + export menu components
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/AddRowForm.tsx (field-per-column form calling api.insertRow, validating required/PK fields) and web/src/components/ExportMenu.tsx (client-side CSV + JSON export of given columns/rows, pure/read-only). Both prop-driven.
acceptanceCriteria: AddRowForm submits typed values via api.insertRow and reports errors inline
ExportMenu produces valid CSV (quotes embedded commas/quotes/newlines) and JSON
ExportMenu handles null cells and an empty row set without crashing

tasks.title: Inline row edit / delete + add/export in TablePreview
tasks.description: Modify web/src/components/TablePreview.tsx: add an optional `primaryKey?: string[]` prop (passed by TableBrowser in TASK-018); editable cells (save via api.updateRow keyed by primaryKey when present, else the full original row values), per-row delete via ConfirmDialog + api.deleteRow, and render <AddRowForm> + <ExportMenu>. On any successful write call useUndo().recordUndo(undoSnapshotId) and refetch the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Editing a cell and saving calls api.updateRow with a PK-based (or full-row) where and refreshes
Deleting a row goes through ConfirmDialog and calls api.deleteRow
Every successful write records an undo entry (banner appears)

tasks.title: SQL console view
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/SqlConsole.tsx: a textarea + Run (engine picker), posting via api.executeSql behind ConfirmDialog; render the result grid (columns/rows) or command tag, and errors. On success record an undo entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Running a SELECT renders a result grid; a write shows the command tag + row count
Running is gated by a confirm step and records an undo entry on success
A SQL error surfaces BwApiError.message without crashing the view

tasks.title: Truncate / drop actions on the table list
tasks.description: Modify web/src/components/TableBrowser.tsx: add per-table Truncate and Drop actions that go through ConfirmDialog and call api.truncateTable/api.dropTable, then refresh the table list and record an undo entry. ALSO thread the selected table's primary key to the preview: pass primaryKey={selectedTable.primaryKey} to <TablePreview> (whose prop is added in TASK-016) so inline edits target the PK.
acceptanceCriteria: Truncate/drop each require confirmation before calling the API
After a successful op the table list refreshes and an undo entry is recorded
A failed op surfaces an error and leaves the list intact
TableBrowser passes the selected table's primaryKey to TablePreview

tasks.title: App integration — SQL tab, Actions, Undo provider
tasks.description: Modify web/src/App.tsx: add a SQL tab rendering <SqlConsole>, mount the existing Actions + ConfirmDialog (snapshot/branch/checkout/delete) in the sidebar/header, wrap the app in UndoProvider and render <UndoBanner>. Refetch /api/state after any branch op.
acceptanceCriteria: A SQL tab is present and renders the console; the Snapshots/Tables/Diff tabs still work
Snapshot/branch/checkout/delete buttons are mounted and call their endpoints (checkout/delete via ConfirmDialog)
The UndoBanner appears app-wide after any mutating action

tasks.title: Unit tests — Postgres mutate builders (mocked exec)
tasks.description: Create test/adapters/postgres-mutate.test.ts: mock exec; assert INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE/DROP build with quoted identifiers + safely-quoted values, an injection-y value is quoted not executed, an empty where is refused, and execute parses CSV results vs command tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Asserts identifiers are quoteIdent'd and values safely quoted/typed (incl. NULL -> IS NULL in WHERE)
A malicious value string is quoted as a literal, never breaks out
updateRow/deleteRow with empty where throws

tasks.title: Unit tests — orchestrator mutate + restoreSnapshot (FakeAdapter)
tasks.description: Create test/core/orchestrator-mutate.test.ts: a FakeMutableAdapter (in-memory) proves the mutate passthrough + restoreSnapshot are engine-agnostic with NO Postgres: each mutate delegates; a non-mutable engine errors; restoreSnapshot takes a safety autosave then restores to the target snapshot's ids; unknown snapshot throws.
acceptanceCriteria: Mutate passthrough + restoreSnapshot work via the fake adapter (no Postgres)
A non-mutable engine produces the typed 'not support writes' error
restoreSnapshot records a safety autosave before restoring; unknown id throws

tasks.title: Server endpoint tests — mutations + restore
tasks.description: Create test/server/server-mutate.test.ts: boot createBwServer with a fake orchestrator; assert every write endpoint requires confirm:true (400 + orchestrator untouched without it), requires the token (401), update/delete reject empty where, each success returns undoSnapshotId + state; POST /api/restore round-trips.
acceptanceCriteria: Each write endpoint returns 400 without confirm and never calls the orchestrator then
A request without the token returns 401
Success responses include undoSnapshotId and refreshed state

tasks.title: Gated integration — real Postgres mutate + undo round trip
tasks.description: Create test/integration/postgres-mutate.int.test.ts (gated on BW_TEST_PG_URL, skips when unset): seed a table, then via the orchestrator insert/update/delete a row, truncate, and run a SQL write — each preceded by a snapshot — then restoreSnapshot and assert the data returns to the pre-action state.
acceptanceCriteria: With a real DB, insert/update/delete/truncate change rows as expected
restoreSnapshot rolls a mutated table back to its pre-action contents
The suite SKIPS (not fails) when BW_TEST_PG_URL is unset

tasks.title: Web component tests — mutations + undo
tasks.description: Create web/src/components/__tests__/mutations.test.tsx: against a mocked api client, assert inline edit/delete call the right methods through ConfirmDialog, AddRowForm submits, ExportMenu emits valid CSV/JSON, SqlConsole renders results, and UndoBanner appears after a recorded undo and calls api.restore.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit/delete/add invoke the mocked api methods only after confirmation where required
ExportMenu output is valid CSV (escaped) and JSON
UndoBanner shows after a mutation and triggers api.restore on click

tasks.title: bw ui mutate e2e smoke (gated)
tasks.description: Create test/e2e/ui-mutate.test.ts (gated on BW_TEST_PG_URL; skips when unset): spawn bw ui, then via HTTP insert a row (token + confirm), assert it is present, POST /api/restore with the returned undoSnapshotId, and assert the row is gone — the full auto-snapshot/undo loop through the running server.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert via the running server then undo restores the prior state (gated on a DB)
A write without confirm/token is rejected by the running server
The suite SKIPS without BW_TEST_PG_URL; the spawned process exits cleanly

tasks.title: Docs — Table actions + MutableAdapter capability
tasks.description: Update README.md (a Table actions section: edit/insert/delete, truncate/drop, SQL console, export, and the auto-snapshot Undo safety model) and docs/ADAPTERS.md (document the OPTIONAL MutableAdapter capability — methods to implement to light up actions, and that omitting it is fine and read-only-safe).
acceptanceCriteria: README documents all four action sets + the Undo/auto-snapshot model
ADAPTERS.md documents MutableAdapter as opt-in (engines without it are read-only)
Docs note every write requires confirmation and is token-gated
